revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''欝色謎命'''（うつしこめのみこと、生年不詳 - [[開化天皇]]元年1月4日（前157年2月11日）以降）は、[[孝元天皇]]の[[皇后...'
revision.text:
'''欝色謎命'''（うつしこめのみこと、生年不詳 - [[開化天皇]]元年1月4日（前157年2月11日）以降）は、[[孝元天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には内色許売命（読み方同じ）とある。父は[[大水口宿禰|大水口宿禰命]]（大矢口宿禰命とも）で、母は[[坂戸由良都姫]]。同母兄に[[欝色雄命]]がいる。[[大綜麻杵命]]（[[伊賀色雄命]]・[[伊賀色謎命]]の父）の同母姉（妹）。[[大彦命]]・開化天皇の母。
孝元天皇7年2月2日（前208年3月20日）、皇后に立てられた。開化天皇元年1月4日（前157年2月11日）、[[皇太后]]となった。
== 系譜 == *父方の祖父：[[出石心大臣命]]（[[饒速日命]]の3世孫乃至4世孫） *父：大水口宿禰命 *母：坂戸由良都姫 *同母兄弟：欝色雄命・大綜麻杵命 *夫：孝元天皇 **子：大彦命・開化天皇・少彦男心命・[[倭迹迹姫命]] **孫：[[御間城姫]]（大彦命の女。[[崇神天皇]]の皇后。[[垂仁天皇]]の母）・崇神天皇・[[彦坐王]]（以上開化天皇の[[皇子]]）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこめのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]]

孝元天皇7年2月2日（前208年3月20日）、皇后に立てられた。開化天皇元年1月4日（前157年2月11日）、[[皇太后]]となった。

{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこめのみこと}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]]

revision.comment: 坂戸由良都姫命について追記。父について出石心大臣命とする情報を脚注で加えたが、大矢口宿禰命とする根拠（脚注）が不明。今後に期待。

'''[[開化天皇]]'''（第9代） | 父親 = [[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]] | 母親 = [[坂戸由良都姫]] | 役職 = | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''欝色謎命'''（うつしこめのみこと、生年不詳 - [[開化天皇]]元年1月4日（紀元前157年2月11日）以降）は、[[孝元天皇]]の[[皇后]][http://ci.nii.ac.jp/naid/40006996348 NII - 欝色謎命]。[[古事記]]には内色許売命（読み方同じ）とある。父は[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]]（大水口宿禰命とも）で、母は[[坂戸由良都姫命]]。同母兄に[[欝色雄命]]（[[穂積氏]]祖）がいる。[[大綜麻杵命]]（[[伊香色雄命]]・[[伊香色謎命]]の父）の同母姉（妹）。[[大彦命]]・開化天皇の母。 孝元天皇7年2月2日（紀元前208年3月20日）、皇后に立てられた。開化天皇元年1月4日（紀元前157年2月11日）、[[皇太后]]となった。 == 系譜 == * 父方の祖父：[[出石心大臣命]]（[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の3世孫乃至4世孫） * 父：[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]][[穂積氏]]の略系譜には、父は出石心大臣命とある。また、坂戸由良都姫命は三上氏家系図にも輿石氏家系図にも、出石心大臣命の妻とある。 * 母：[[坂戸由良都姫命]]（[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の5代孫、[[川枯彦命]]の娘、[[三上氏]]族）[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301765/84 御上祝家系図（三上氏）（コマ番号84-97）/諸系譜. 第28冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301743/84 天津彦根命庶流　三枝部造　甲斐野呂氏後裔　輿石氏家系図（コマ番号84-92）　/諸系譜. 第6冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション * 同母兄弟：[[欝色雄命]]・[[大綜麻杵命]] * 夫：[[孝元天皇]] ** 子：[[大彦命]]・[[開化天皇]]・[[少彦男心命]]・[[倭迹迹姫命]] ** 孫：[[御間城姫]]（大彦命の女。[[崇神天皇]]の皇后。[[垂仁天皇]]の母）・崇神天皇・[[彦坐王]]（以上、開化天皇の[[皇子]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこめのみこと}} [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

[[開化天皇]] |養子女 = |皇居 = |空欄表題1 = 異表記 |空欄記載1 = 内色許売命（古事記） |脚注 = }} '''欝色謎命'''（うつしこめのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[孝元天皇]]の[[皇后]][http://ci.nii.ac.jp/naid/40006996348 NII - 欝色謎命]。[[古事記]]には内色許売命（読み方同じ）とある。先代旧事本紀によると、父は[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]]（大水口宿禰命とも）で、母は[[坂戸由良都姫命]]。同母兄に[[欝色雄命]]（[[穂積氏]]祖）がいる。[[大綜麻杵命]]（[[伊香色雄命]]・[[伊香色謎命]]の父）の同母姉（妹）。[[大彦命]]・開化天皇の母。 孝元天皇7年2月2日、皇后に立てられた。開化天皇元年1月4日、[[皇太后]]となった。 == 系譜 == * 父方の祖父：[[出石心大臣命]]（[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の3世孫乃至4世孫） * 父：[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]][[穂積氏]]の略系譜には、父は出石心大臣命とある。また、坂戸由良都姫命は三上氏家系図にも輿石氏家系図にも、出石心大臣命の妻とある。 * 母：[[坂戸由良都姫命]]（[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の5代孫、[[川枯彦命]]の娘、[[三上氏]]族）[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301765/84 御上祝家系図（三上氏）（コマ番号84-97）/諸系譜. 第28冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301743/84 天津彦根命庶流　三枝部造　甲斐野呂氏後裔　輿石氏家系図（コマ番号84-92）　/諸系譜. 第6冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション * 同母兄弟：[[欝色雄命]]・[[大綜麻杵命]] * 夫：[[孝元天皇]] ** 子：[[大彦命]]・[[開化天皇]]・[[少彦男心命]]・[[倭迹迹姫命]] ** 孫：[[御間城姫]]（大彦命の女。[[崇神天皇]]の皇后。[[垂仁天皇]]の母）・崇神天皇・[[彦坐王]]（以上、開化天皇の[[皇子]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこめのみこと}} [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:D]][[ja:欝色謎命]] ]]

{{Infobox Empress | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortrank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{List of Empresses of Japan}} {{List of Empress Dowagers of Japan}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:D]][[ja:欝色謎命]] ]]

Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{List of Empresses of Japan}} {{List of Empress Dowagers of Japan}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] ]]

In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{List of Empresses of Japan}} {{List of Empress Dowagers of Japan}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Utsushikome]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] ]]

Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Generations of Jimmu that need correction]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[simple:Utsushikome]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Consorts of Japan}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Utsushikome]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Utsushikome]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] ]]

Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]] '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[simple:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]{{Consorts of Japan}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:dd]][[en:Utsushikome]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Utsushikome]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Utsushikome]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] ]]

In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. [[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. [[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortrank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortrank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. [[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Utsushikome-qq]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[simple:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]{{Consorts of Japan}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:dd]][[en:Utsushikome]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Utsushikome]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category{Utsushikome]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[en:Utsushikome]][[ja:欝色謎命]] ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Utsushikome]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Utsushikome]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:tsushikome]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Utsushikome]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortrank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortrank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] * **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ** **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:U completion categories]] [[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ** **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. [[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ** **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: - [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] - [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] - [[Emperor Kaika]] - [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Utsushikome]][[Category:Utsushikome]]'''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortrank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ** **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: ** [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ** [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: ** [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ** [[Emperor Kaika]] ** [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ** [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Utsushikome]]{{Consorts of Japan}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Utsushikome]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Utsushikome]] [[en:Utsushikome]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] [[sn:tsushikome]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome no Mikoto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Utsushikome}}

Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. revision.comment: 36 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:欝色謎命 up to rev 102406381
revision.text:
{{統合文字|謎}} {{基礎情報 皇后 |名 = 欝色謎命 |立后根拠 = 第8代天皇后 |画像 = |画像サイズ = |画像説明 = |画像代替文 = |在位期間 = |和暦在位期間 = |皇后 = 孝元天皇7年2月2日 |皇太后 = 開化天皇元年1月4日 |出生日 = |生地 = |死亡日 = |没地 = |陵所 = |諱 = |諡号 = |異称 = |幼称 = |氏族 = |父親 =[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]] |母親 = [[坂戸由良都姫]] |配偶者1 =[[孝元天皇]] |結婚 = |子女 =[[大彦命]]
[[開化天皇]] |養子女 = |皇居 = |空欄表題1 = 異表記 |空欄記載1 = 内色許売命（古事記） |脚注 = }} '''欝色謎命'''（うつしこめのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[孝元天皇]]の[[皇后]][https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - 欝色謎命] - CiNii Research。『[[日本書紀]]』の表記であり、『[[古事記]]』には「内色許売命（読み方同じ）」とある。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』によると、父は[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]]（[[大水口宿禰|大水口宿禰命]]とも）で、母は[[坂戸由良都姫命]]。同母兄に[[欝色雄命]]（[[穂積氏]]祖）がいる。[[大綜麻杵命]]（[[伊香色雄命]]・[[伊香色謎命]]の父）の同母姉（妹）。[[大彦命]]・開化天皇の母。 孝元天皇7年2月2日、皇后に立てられた。開化天皇元年1月4日、[[皇太后]]となった。 == 系譜 == * 父方の祖父：[[出石心大臣命]]（[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の3世孫乃至4世孫） * 父：[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]][[穂積氏]]の略系譜には、父は出石心大臣命とある。また、坂戸由良都姫命は三上氏家系図にも輿石氏家系図にも、出石心大臣命の妻とある。 * 母：[[坂戸由良都姫命]]（[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の5代孫、[[川枯彦命]]の娘、[[三上氏]]族）[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301765/84 御上祝家系図（三上氏）（コマ番号84-97）/諸系譜. 第28冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301743/84 天津彦根命庶流　三枝部造　甲斐野呂氏後裔　輿石氏家系図（コマ番号84-92）　/諸系譜. 第6冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション * 同母兄弟：[[欝色雄命]]・[[大綜麻杵命]] * 夫：[[孝元天皇]] ** 子：[[大彦命]]・[[開化天皇]]・[[少彦男心命]]・[[倭迹迹姫命]] ** 孫：[[御間城姫]]（大彦命の女。[[崇神天皇]]の皇后。[[垂仁天皇]]の母）・崇神天皇・[[彦坐王]]（以上、開化天皇の[[皇子]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[穂積氏]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこめのみこと}} [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]
