[[開化天皇]] |養子女 = |皇居 = |空欄表題1 = 異表記 |空欄記載1 = 内色許売命（古事記） |脚注 = }} '''欝色謎命'''（うつしこめのみこと、生没年不詳）は、[[孝元天皇]]の[[皇后]][https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - 欝色謎命] - CiNii Research。『[[日本書紀]]』の表記であり、『[[古事記]]』には「内色許売命（読み方同じ）」とある。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』によると、父は[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]]（[[大水口宿禰|大水口宿禰命]]とも）で、母は[[坂戸由良都姫命]]。同母兄に[[欝色雄命]]（[[穂積氏]]祖）がいる。[[大綜麻杵命]]（[[伊香色雄命]]・[[伊香色謎命]]の父）の同母姉（妹）。[[大彦命]]・開化天皇の母。 孝元天皇7年2月2日、皇后に立てられた。開化天皇元年1月4日、[[皇太后]]となった。 == 系譜 == * 父方の祖父：[[出石心大臣命]]（[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の3世孫乃至4世孫） * 父：[[大矢口宿禰|大矢口宿禰命]][[穂積氏]]の略系譜には、父は出石心大臣命とある。また、坂戸由良都姫命は三上氏家系図にも輿石氏家系図にも、出石心大臣命の妻とある。 * 母：[[坂戸由良都姫命]]（[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の5代孫、[[川枯彦命]]の娘、[[三上氏]]族）[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301765/84 御上祝家系図（三上氏）（コマ番号84-97）/諸系譜. 第28冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301743/84 天津彦根命庶流　三枝部造　甲斐野呂氏後裔　輿石氏家系図（コマ番号84-92）　/諸系譜. 第6冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション * 同母兄弟：[[欝色雄命]]・[[大綜麻杵命]] * 夫：[[孝元天皇]] ** 子：[[大彦命]]・[[開化天皇]]・[[少彦男心命]]・[[倭迹迹姫命]] ** 孫：[[御間城姫]]（大彦命の女。[[崇神天皇]]の皇后。[[垂仁天皇]]の母）・崇神天皇・[[彦坐王]]（以上、開化天皇の[[皇子]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[穂積氏]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うつしこめのみこと}} [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ** **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: ** [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ** [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: ** [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ** [[Emperor Kaika]] ** [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ** [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== [[simple:Utsushikome]]{{Consorts of Japan}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Utsushikome]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Utsushikome]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Utsushikome]] [[en:Utsushikome]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] [[sn:tsushikome]] {{citation needed|Utsushikome no Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Utsushikome}} {{translated page|ja|欝色謎命|version=102406381|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == Lineage == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * **Father**: [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ** **Paternal grandfather**: [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * **Mother**: [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * **Siblings**: ** [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ** [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * **Spouse**: [[Emperor Kōgen]] * **Children**: ** [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ** [[Emperor Kaika]] ** [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ** [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == Historical Record == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == Related Figures == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = {{Consorts of Japan}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[empress]] of [[Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = {{Consorts of Japan}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Year of birth {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11544859]] Wikidata name: Empress Utsusikome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548}}; {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|qid=Q11435420}}; {{ill|Yamatototohime no mikoto|qid=Q11387096}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|qid=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q11436785|qid=Q11436785}}|mother={{ill|Q106106938|qid=Q106106938}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11544859]] Wikidata name: Empress Utsusikome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548}}; {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|qid=Q11435420}}; {{ill|Yamatototohime no mikoto|qid=Q11387096}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|qid=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q11436785|qid=Q11436785}}|mother={{ill|Q106106938|qid=Q106106938}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11544859]] Wikidata name: Empress Utsusikome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. [[en:Utsushikome]][[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]][[ja:欝色謎命]][[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]][[zh:郁色谜命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548}}; {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}; {{ill|Yamatototohime no mikoto|WD=Q11387096}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q11436785|WD=Q11436785}}|mother={{ill|Q106106938|WD=Q106106938}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11544859]] Wikidata name: Empress Utsusikome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. [[en:Utsushikome]][[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]][[ja:欝色謎命]][[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]][[zh:郁色谜命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548}}; {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}; {{ill|Yamatototohime no mikoto|WD=Q11387096}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q11436785|WD=Q11436785}}|mother={{ill|Q106106938|WD=Q106106938}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11544859]] Wikidata name: Empress Utsusikome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = {{Consorts of Japan}} {{translated page|ja|欝色謎命|version=102406381|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:Utsushikome]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] [[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]] [[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]] [[zh:郁色谜命]] {{wikidata link|Q11544859}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

[[en:Utsushikome]][[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]][[ja:欝色謎命]][[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]][[zh:郁色谜命]] [[en:Utsushikome]][[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]][[ja:欝色謎命]][[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]][[zh:郁色谜命]] [[en:Utsushikome]][[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]][[ja:欝色謎命]][[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]][[zh:郁色谜命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548}}; {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}; {{ill|Yamatototohime no mikoto|WD=Q11387096}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q11436785|WD=Q11436785}}|mother={{ill|Q106106938|WD=Q106106938}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11544859]] Wikidata name: Empress Utsusikome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the [[:en:Empress|Empress]] of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]], as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]] ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]]'s reign. Upon the accession of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], she assumed the title of [[:en:Empress dowager|Empress dowager]]. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]] * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = {{Consorts of Japan}} {{translated page|ja|欝色謎命|version=102406381|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:Utsushikome]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] [[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]] [[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]] [[zh:郁色谜命]] {{wikidata link|Q11544859}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

[[en:Utsushikome]][[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]][[ja:欝色謎命]][[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]][[zh:郁色谜命]] [[en:Utsushikome]][[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]][[ja:欝色謎命]][[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]][[zh:郁色谜命]] [[en:Utsushikome]][[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]][[ja:欝色謎命]][[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]][[zh:郁色谜命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548}}; {{ill|Prince Ōhiko|WD=Q11435420}}; {{ill|Yamatototohime no mikoto|WD=Q11387096}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q11436785|WD=Q11436785}}|mother={{ill|Q106106938|WD=Q106106938}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11544859]] Wikidata name: Empress Utsusikome [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Utsushikome no Mikoto | imperial_consortRank = Empress of the 8th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_text = | reign = | era_reign = | consort = February 2, 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}'s reign | empress_dowager = January 4, 1st year of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}'s reign | birth_date = | birth_place = | death_date = | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | alternate_titles = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | childhood_name = | clan = | father = [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune|Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] | mother = [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]] | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}} | marriage = | children = [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}} | adopted_children = | imperial_residence = | blank_label1 = Alternate Names | blank_data1 = Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命, ''Kojiki'') | notes = }} '''Utsushikome no Mikoto''' (欝色謎命, also read Uchishikome no Mikoto) was the {{ill|Empress|en|Empress|WD=Q100387742|lt=Empress}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}, as recorded in historical texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' and ''[[Kojiki]]''.[https://cir.nii.ac.jp/crid/1520854805362042752 NII - Utsushikome no Mikoto] - CiNii Research She is described as the mother of [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] and {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}. Her name in the ''Kojiki'' is recorded as Uchishikome no Mikoto (内色許売命). == HEADING == Lineage == HEADING == Utsushikome is said to be the daughter of [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]], also called Ōmizuguchi no Sukune no Mikoto, and [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]]. She is related to the [[Hozumi clan]] as the sister of [[Utsushio no Mikoto]], a clan ancestor. She is also noted to be the older sister of [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]], the father of [[Ikashikohime no Mikoto]] (also referred to as [[Ikashiko no Mikoto]]), mother to key descendants in Japanese mythology. Her genealogical details are as follows: * '''Father''': [[Ōyakuchi no Sukune no Mikoto]] ''' '''Paternal grandfather''': [[Izushi Kokoro no Omochi no Mikoto]], purportedly a great-grandson of [[Nigihayahi no Mikoto]] * '''Mother''': [[Sakadoyura Tsuhime]], daughter of [[Kawakarehiko no Mikoto]] and a descendant of [[Ameno Mikage no Kami]] * '''Siblings''': ''' [[Utsushio no Mikoto]] ''' [[Ōsomasuki no Mikoto]] * '''Spouse''': {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}} * '''Children''': ''' [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]] ''' {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}} ''' [[Shōhiko no Mikoto]] ''' [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]] == HEADING == Historical Record == HEADING == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' Utsushikome was made empress on the 2nd day of the 2nd month in the 7th year of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}'s reign. Upon the accession of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}, she assumed the title of {{ill|Empress dowager|en|Empress dowager|WD=Q7409248|lt=Empress dowager}}. In some genealogies, her alternate names or associated figures such as Uchishikome or variations related to her parentage are noted, emphasizing her association with the [[Hozumi clan]] and broader aristocratic lineages. == HEADING == Related Figures == HEADING == Utsushikome’s descendants include notable figures in Japanese mythology and history: - [[Ōbiko no Mikoto]]: A progenitor figure linked to later imperial lineages. - {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}: The 9th Emperor of Japan. - [[Yamatototohime no Mikoto]]: An influential female figure in early imperial genealogies. == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == {{Reflist}} == HEADING == See Also == HEADING == * [[Hozumi clan]] * {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}} * [[Japanese mythology]] {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} {{Imperial-stub}} == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = '''Utsushikome''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. == HEADING == Genealogy == HEADING == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == HEADING == Footnotes == HEADING == == HEADING == =Notes == HEADING == = == HEADING == =References == HEADING == = {{Consorts of Japan}} {{translated page|ja|欝色謎命|version=102406381|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:Utsushikome]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] [[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]] [[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]] [[zh:郁色谜命]] {{wikidata link|Q11544859}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

Princess Yamatototo-hime | father = Oyaguchi Sukune | mother = Sakado Yuramiyakohime }} '''Utsushikome''' (欝色謎命) was [[empress consort of Japan]] from 208 BC to 157 BC, and empress dowager from 157 BC.Anston, p. 148 (Vol. 1)Anston, p. 149 (Vol. 1) Her father was a man named Oyaguchi Sukune and her mother was a woman named Sakado Yuramiyakohime.{{Cite web |title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/10301765/1/84 |access-date=2025-09-11 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp}}{{Cite web |title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/10301743/1/84 |access-date=2025-09-11 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp}} == Life == She was the wife of [[Emperor Kōgen]]. However little information exists about her. Her husband belongs to a group of Emperor's known as "[[Nihon Shoki#Kesshi Hachidai|Kesshi-hachidai]]" or "eight generations lacking history".Aston. W.G. (1896) “Nihongi Volume 1: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD697”. Tuttle Publishing. As such there is little information about her and her husband. However, both her parents are mentioned whereas typically only the fathers of Empress Consorts were ever recorded (if any of the parents were recorded in the first place). == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | before=[[Kuwashi-hime]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=208–157 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Kuwashi-hime]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=appointed in 157 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{authority control}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[simple:Utsushikome]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] [[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]] [[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]] [[zh:郁色谜命]] {{wikidata link|Q11544859}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

However, both her parents are mentioned whereas typically only the fathers of Empress Consorts were ever recorded (if any of the parents were recorded in the first place). == Family tree == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == Notes == {{reflist|group=lower-alpha}} == References == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | before=[[Kuwashi-hime]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=208–157 BC}} {{succession box | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | before=[[Kuwashi-hime]] | after=[[Ikagashikome]] | years=appointed in 157 BC}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{authority control}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[simple:Utsushikome]] [[ja:欝色謎命]] [[fa:اوتسوشی کومه نو میکوتو]] [[ko:우쓰시코메노미코토]] [[zh:郁色谜命]] {{wikidata link|Q11544859}} [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Mothers of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
